text: Built out the `working` SLR living-workspace by dogfooding a real systematic literature review on how genetic mutations in mice affect malignant mesothelioma susceptibility after asbestos exposure. The bulk of the work was making the process transparent and auditable: generating an HTML report that walks through every query, filter, and inclusion/exclusion step so the exact methodology is reproducible. Turned the repo into a reusable instance by adding a reset/new-instance capability and authored a README in WORKING that frames the system around SLR visibility. Fixed a real bug where views were being emitted to the views folder instead of the per-study folder, clarifying that a view has two parts, and pinned down the distinction between "build report" and "build views." Ended on a documentation-design discussion of how a fresh Claude session is meant to discover and correctly use these tools.
